sections.paragraph:
σύμβιος, ον ‘living together’ (Aristot. et al.; TestJud 23:3; TestIss 7:2 v.l. [for γυναικός]; Philo, Poster. Cai. 78) subst. ‘companion’ then esp. ὁ, ἡ ς. husband, wife (ins and oft. in pap; New Docs 1 no. 18, 18) IPol 5:1 MMacDonald, NTS 40, ’94, 105–25; Hv 2, 2, 3.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

esp. esp. = especially

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

oft. oft. = often

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

Hv Hv = Visions

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 957
